name: bcrypt (Password Hashing for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); pyca / Python Cryptographic Authority

auth.notes: Password hashing library; no external auth

performance.notes: Intentionally slow by design; rounds parameter controls cost (default 12)

agent_readiness.notes: Bcrypt password hashing. import bcrypt. password = b'my_password'. hashed = bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt()). bcrypt.checkpw(password, hashed) → True/False. bcrypt.gensalt(rounds=12) → random salt. rounds: 4-31; each increment doubles time. hashed is bytes: b'$2b$12$...'. Type annotations. C extension in 4.x via Rust. bcrypt.hashpw() is blocking — run in executor for async. Password must be bytes — encode string first: password.encode(). Max password length: 72 bytes (silently truncates beyond that). Used with FastAPI: await asyncio.to_thread(bcrypt.checkpw, password, hashed). Production standard for password storage.

gotchas: Max 72 bytes — passwords longer than 72 bytes are silently truncated
Blocking I/O — must use asyncio.to_thread() in async contexts
Password must be bytes — encode str first
Higher rounds = more secure but slower — balance for your hardware
For new systems, argon2-cffi is now recommended over bcrypt
best_when: Password storage in any Python web application
When NIST/OWASP compliance requires adaptive hashing
avoid_when: New systems — prefer argon2-cffi (winner of Password Hashing Competition)
Passwords longer than 72 bytes without pre-hashing strategy
High-volume async password checking without thread executor
summary: bcrypt provides password hashing for Python. bcrypt.hashpw(password, bcrypt.gensalt(rounds=12)). bcrypt.checkpw(password, hashed). Bytes only — encode strings. 72-byte max (silent truncation). C extension via Rust in v4. Apache-2.0 by pyca. Blocking — use asyncio.to_thread() for async. Industry standard. For new systems, prefer argon2-cffi (PHC winner). Adjust rounds for your server's target latency (200-500ms).
